content: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS) uses magnetic fields to stimulate specific brain regions non-invasively. FDA-approved for treatment-resistant depression (since 2008), OCD, migraine, and smoking cessation. Standard protocol: high-frequency (10Hz) stimulation of left dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (DLPFC) daily for 4-6 weeks. Response rates ~50-60%, remission ~30%. Theta burst stimulation (iTBS) delivers treatment in 3 minutes vs 37 minutes with comparable efficacy. Side effects are mild (scalp discomfort, headache); seizure risk is extremely low (<0.1%). TMS is covered by most insurers after 2-4 medication failures. Deep TMS coils reach medial prefrontal and anterior cingulate regions.
